Overview

Address
832 Sicotte Street, L' Anse, MI 49946
Phone
9065246531
Certified beds
59
Avg daily residents
54 (91% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1977-12-01
Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.23 3.18 3.93 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.76 0.56 0.93 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.57 0.71 0.70 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.90 1.92 2.30 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.34 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.02 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.81
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.54
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.42
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
New facility — insufficient data.
RN turnover
New facility — insufficient data.
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.13
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.82
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-09-10 22 18 6 291 2 437
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-07-11 19 16 3 180 1 180
Total weighted health score
372.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (47)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
42 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2025-10-08 2025-11-21
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-09-10 2025-11-06
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. E 2025-09-10 2025-11-06
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-09-10 2025-12-22
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0711 Ensure the resident's doctor reviews the resident's care, writes, signs and dates progress notes and orders, at each required visit. D 2025-09-10 2025-12-22
0712 Ensure that the resident and his/her doctor meet face-to-face at all required visits. D 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. F 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. F 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-09-10 2025-12-22
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. F 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly F 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. F 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2025-09-10 2025-10-13
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. G 2025-05-07 2025-08-08
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-05-07 2025-06-05
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2025-05-07 2025-06-05
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0711 Ensure the resident's doctor reviews the resident's care, writes, signs and dates progress notes and orders, at each required visit. E 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. F 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. F 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. F 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. D 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly F 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. C 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. F 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0949 Provide behavior health training consistent with the requirements and as determined by a facility assessment. F 2024-07-11 2024-08-10
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-04-22 2024-05-17
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2023-07-17 2023-08-15
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2023-07-17 2023-08-15
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-07-17 2023-08-15
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2023-07-17 2023-08-15
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2023-07-17 2023-08-15
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2023-07-17 2023-08-15
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-06-09 2023-07-10
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2023-06-09 2023-07-10
